Navigation.menuDescription: Mobile navigation, language, and media controls.

Home.title: Creative + Developer.
Home.subtitle: Software, games, visual systems. Built deliberately from Ontario — one archive across engineering, art, and writing.

compass.heading: Find your way around.
compass.body: I'm Mark — a creative developer in Ontario, working across software, games, and visual systems. This site is one archive instead of seven scattered profiles.

logs.label: Logs
logs.description: Devlogs, field notes, things I'm thinking about.

resources.label: Resources
resources.description: Tools I built, links I trust.

games.heading: In the workshop.
games.lede: Prototypes and engine studies — systems, input, and game feel. Most are still cooking; here's what's on the bench.

visual.heading: Frames & films.
visual.lede: Black-and-white photography, digital art, and short films. Stills and motion, filed as they're finished.
visual.framesSoon: Frames are being edited — the structure's ready, the images land next.

resources.heading: Tools & links.
resources.lede: A working stack — in-browser dev utilities I built, plus the links I actually keep open. Each with a note on why.

aboutPreview.heading: The person behind it.
aboutPreview.lede: A cross-cultural path, Ontario-based. Software, games, and images — one practice across many surfaces.

aboutBriefly.heading: Mark, briefly.
aboutBriefly.subnote: Cross-cultural path. Ontario-based. Operates one archive instead of seven scattered profiles.

selectedWork.heading: Things I shipped.
selectedWork.lede: A short loop of the projects worth showing — ready ones in front, drafts dimmed behind them.

frames.heading: How I got here.
frames.lede: A short visual journal — the rooms, the years, the moments that earned a slot.
frames.firstComputer: First computer — discovering you can make the screen do things.
frames.firstGame: First game project — wrote a story before I wrote the engine.
frames.moveToCanada: China to Canada — a long flight and a new accent.
frames.westernUniversity: Western University — software engineering, late labs, a city that taught me to ship.
frames.jdPower: J.D. Power — full-stack internship, real users, real tickets.
frames.firstFilm: First short film — handheld, mostly natural light.
frames.openingTheArchive: Opening the archive — this site, structured for the long run.

letsBuild.body: Something on your mind? Frontend systems, prototypes, creative tooling, or just a hello — whichever.

capabilities.title: Systems & surfaces
capabilities.lede: Five overlapping systems. Each is a real practice, not a buzzword.

production.title: Production engineering
production.description: Shipping Next.js / TypeScript / CI / observability work — the slow, careful kind that survives the second year.

interface.title: Interface systems
interface.description: Design systems, tokens, motion, Storybook, accessibility — interfaces you can read end-to-end.

game.title: Game feel & interaction
game.description: Prototypes, input systems, tuning. LAST KERNEL and a small workshop of interactive ideas.

ai.title: AI-assisted creative tools
ai.description: Agent workflows, content pipelines, internal tooling. Models treated as craft, not as a brand.

visual.title: Visual storytelling
visual.description: Photography, film, art direction, gallery curation — frames that earn their place.

Home.featuredDescription: A few production-grade case studies across web apps, AI tooling, and data visualisation.
Home.latestDescription: Short, dated, opinionated. Devlog plus systems notes — syndicated from dev.to.
Home.toolsDescription: A small, real personal stack — the things I actually use, with notes on why.

Home.closingStatement: One archive. Software, games, art, writing — built deliberately.

About.title: About
About.intro: A quiet profile of Mark Yu: software, games, images, and the archive in progress.

refined.heroBody: Ontario, by way of China. I make small digital things: interfaces, game-feel experiments, tools, images, notes. The rest is intentionally left quiet.
refined.toolsBody: A short version. The rest can stay off-screen.

toolDescriptions.data: Where things get stored, shaped, and moved.

refined.closingBody: The useful proof is in the work. The human part is in the message.

character.heroBody: Ontario, by way of China. Code, game feel, quiet images. Mostly building a small world and keeping the useful parts visible.
character.profileBody: No lore dump. Just enough signal: interfaces, prototypes, photos, notes.
character.questBody: Making this place feel less like a portfolio and more like a room.
character.loadoutBody: Not a trophy wall. Just the current desk.
character.closingBody: The rest is in the work, or in a message.

bio.paragraph1: Mark is a software engineer from Ontario, Canada — by way of China. He builds web applications by day and tinkers with games, shaders, and black-and-white photography the rest of the time.
bio.paragraph2: Trained at Western University, sharpened by a full-stack co-op at J.D. Power. He treats portfolio surfaces like product surfaces: tight type, fast load, no fake metrics.
bio.paragraph3: When he isn't shipping he's usually somewhere with a camera. The travel log to the right is roughly chronological.

Changelog.empty: No release notes are available yet. Check back soon.

app.title: Application core
app.body: Next.js App Router, React, and TypeScript shape the public routes, admin surfaces, and server-rendered content.

interface.title: Interface layer
interface.body: Tailwind tokens, Radix primitives, and restrained motion carry the glass HUD and archive/pixel visual language.

data.title: Content and data
data.body: Zod-validated content falls back cleanly while Supabase powers accounts, saves, comments, and admin edits.

commerce.title: Commerce and email
commerce.body: Stripe handles checkout paths, Resend handles transactional mail, and Vercel keeps previews close to production.

quality.title: Quality gates
quality.body: ESLint, TypeScript, Playwright, and Storybook cover the pieces that need to keep behaving as the site grows.

delivery.title: Delivery surface
delivery.body: ISR pages, route metadata, sitemap generation, and OG image handlers keep the archive crawlable and shareable.

Colophon.auditBody: The changelog mirrors the repository history in a visitor-readable form, without exposing private project notes.

Meta.galleryDescription: Black-and-white photography, digital art, and contact sheets — visual archive by ZhenXiao Mark Yu.
Meta.resourcesDescription: Interactive in-browser tools plus a curated set of daily-use developer links. Pick a side: hands-on utilities, or the bookmarks I actually open.
Meta.resourcesToolsDescription: Thirty-eight interactive tools — JSON formatter, contrast checker, JWT decoder, password generator, and more — that run in your browser. No login, no upload, no tracking.
Meta.resourcesLinksDescription: Curated bookmarks for docs, AI assistants, color tools, hosting, playgrounds, and inspiration — the ones I actually open every day.
Meta.contactDescription: Project inquiries and collaboration. Mailto and form fallback now, server-side provider later.
Meta.mediaDescription: Video and process archive — draft frames hold the layout while final clips and posters land.
Meta.shopDescription: Limited prints, posters, and small editions — the storefront opens once the first run is ready.
Meta.notesDescription: Short-form notes — design fragments, working ideas, and reading marginalia, kept terse and dated.
Meta.latestDescription: A single feed of new notes, logs, work, frames, resources, and shop pieces across the archive.
Meta.changelogDescription: Public release notes and repository changes for m4rkyu.com.
Meta.colophonDescription: The stack, systems, and craft decisions behind this Next.js portfolio.
Meta.topicsDescription: Browse every project, note, and tool by shared topic.
Meta.searchDescription: Search every project, note, tool, and frame across m4rkyu.com.

Auth.signInDescription: Optional. Lets you save things and leave comments. Nothing else.

Auth.magicLinkSentBody: Sent a sign-in link to {email}. Usually arrives in a few seconds — tap it to finish, or paste the 6–8 digit code below if the link doesn't open.

Auth.confirmSentBody: Sent a confirmation link to {email}. Tap it to finish setting up your account.

AdminNotes.linkLabelHint: Optional text for the link chip; defaults to the hostname.
AdminNotes.tiersHint: One tier per line, e.g. "S: Inter, Berkeley Mono". Used only for tier lists.

AdminGames.title: Games
AdminGames.description: Create and edit game-development archive entries. Public /games falls back to src/content/games.ts when the table is empty.
AdminGames.count: {count, plural, =0 {No games} one {# game} other {# games}}
AdminGames.newGameDescription: Add a game-development archive entry. Save as draft until the build is ready to show.
AdminGames.emptyTitle: No games in the table yet
AdminGames.emptyDescription: Public /games still renders the static src/content/games.ts list. Add a game here to start migrating.
AdminGames.deleteGameConfirm: Delete "{title}"? Public /games falls back to the static array. Cannot be undone.
AdminGames.deleteWarning: Permanently removes this game. Public /games falls back to the static array. Cannot be undone.
AdminGames.slugHint: Lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens. Used in the URL.

AdminMedia.title: Media

AdminMedia.editMediaDescription: Edit this media item's details, status, and ordering.
AdminMedia.emptyTitle: No media in the table yet

AdminMedia.deleteWarning: Deleting removes this item from the media page permanently.

AdminMedia.imageHint: JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, or GIF. Up to 10 MB. Shown on /media instead of the placeholder frame.

AdminMedia.posterAltHint: Describe the poster for screen readers.

AdminProfile.title: Profile
AdminProfile.description: The single source of truth for the /about page — name, intro, socials, and the structured data behind the timeline, skills, and travel map.
AdminProfile.savedHint: Changes go live across the site on save.
AdminProfile.structuredHint: These fields are authored as JSON and validated on save. Keep the shape — a bad entry is rejected with the field name so you know where to fix it.
AdminProfile.jsonHint: Valid JSON, validated against the profile schema on save.
